creator: js@jschneider.com
text:
Found the problem and the fix.  Forgot that I had enabled "device cryptodev" in my kernel conf.  After disabling this, encfs is working again.

I still have "device crypto" enabled in kernel.  From the cryptodev man page, I can't tell the difference between device crypto and device cryptodev.  The man page for aesni says you need both crypto and cryptodev.  I still see the aesni0 device though, so it's not clear what the requirements are.  All I know is that cryptodev breaks encfs.

Closing this issue as it's clearly not an encfs or port problem.
